careers.title: Careers at Billion Towers
careers.subtitle: Building institutional-grade infrastructure for tokenized real estate.
careers.description: We're recruiting experienced builders across engineering, product, compliance, and operations who are passionate about bringing traditional real estate economics onchain. Billion Towers combines fintech expertise with deep real estate knowledge to create a transparent, institutional-grade platform.

sections.heading: Why Billion Towers?
sections.text: Our mission is to democratize access to premium real estate investments while maintaining institutional-grade standards for risk management and transparency. We partner with leading institutions to tokenize verified real estate assets and provide investors with clear ownership, reporting, and liquidity pathways. This is infrastructure work with real-world impact.
items: Work on real assets backed by institutional partnerships and regulatory oversight
Culture of transparency—every decision is grounded in investor protection
Opportunity to shape product direction and market strategy
Competitive compensation with meaningful equity ownership

sections.heading: Open roles
items: Senior Solidity Engineer — Design and audit smart contracts for asset tokenization, custody, and settlement.
Full-Stack Engineer — Own the investor dashboard, marketplace UX, and API infrastructure.
Senior Compliance Officer — Lead AML/KYC frameworks, investor accreditation programs, and regulatory strategy.
Real Estate Operations Manager — Source and evaluate properties, manage asset partnerships.
Product Manager, Institutional — Own partnerships with sponsors and institutional investors.
Security Engineer — Implement custody controls and manage platform security posture.
Data Engineer — Build data pipelines for property performance metrics and investor reporting.

sections.heading: Requirements
items: Demonstrated experience in fintech, banking, or regulated markets (minimum 4+ years)
Deep understanding of blockchain architecture and smart contract best practices
Track record shipping products that handle compliance, security, and user trust
Strong communication skills—ability to translate complex topics for non-technical stakeholders
Alignment with institutional-grade execution and bias toward getting things right

callout.title: Interested?
callout.text: Send your resume, GitHub profile, and a brief note on why you're interested in tokenized real estate to careers@billiontowers.com.

tokenomics.title: Tokenomics
tokenomics.subtitle: A disciplined token economy built around investor value.
tokenomics.description: Our token model is designed to support platform stability, transparent fees, and balanced economic incentives for long-term growth.

sections.heading: Supply and distribution
sections.text: A controlled token supply with reserved allocations for development, liquidity programs, and long-term platform support.
items: Fixed supply schedule
Reserved allocations for product and growth
Liquidity incentives aligned with investor needs

sections.heading: Fees and rewards
sections.text: Fee structures are transparent and designed to support project delivery without eroding investor economics.
items: Marketplace fees kept competitive
Performance-aligned incentives for long-term holders
Clear disclosure of transaction and custody costs

sections.heading: Governance and clarity
sections.text: Token holders receive visibility into operating priorities, product upgrades, and governance commitments.
items: Policy disclosures for token issuance
Regular economics updates
Investor-aligned decision frameworks

risk-disclosure.title: Risk Disclosure
risk-disclosure.subtitle: Understand the risks before investing.
risk-disclosure.description: Real estate investing carries market, liquidity, operational, and regulatory risk. Please review these disclosures carefully before participating in fractional property opportunities.

sections.heading: Market risk
sections.text: Property values can move up or down in response to macroeconomic cycles, interest rates, local demand, and capital market conditions. Market risk may lead to lower valuations and reduced returns, especially during economic downturns.
items: Economic and interest rate shifts
Local supply and demand changes
Sector-specific performance variability

sections.heading: Performance variability
sections.text: Each investment’s return depends on property-level execution, tenant mix, leasing performance, and operational efficiency. Historical outcomes are not a reliable predictor of future performance.
items: Asset selection and underwriting quality
Operating income and occupancy trends
Unexpected repair or cost increases

sections.heading: Liquidity risk
sections.text: Some holdings may be harder to sell quickly depending on marketplace demand, asset type, and market conditions. Limited liquidity can delay exit timing and increase the cost of selling.
items: Secondary market depth may vary
Time required to find a buyer
Price impact of forced sales

sections.heading: Operational risk
sections.text: Properties depend on effective management, maintenance, and tenant performance. Operational challenges such as maintenance issues, tenant defaults, or management disruptions can reduce net returns.
items: Maintenance and capital expenditures
Property management execution
Tenant turnover and vacancy risk

sections.heading: Regulatory and legal risk
sections.text: Changes in zoning, tax rules, compliance requirements, or legal frameworks can affect asset performance and investor returns. Regulatory risk is an inherent part of real estate investment.

callout.title: Invest responsibly
callout.text: Review your objectives, investment horizon, and risk tolerance before participating in fractional property investments.

compliance.title: Compliance & Regulatory Framework
compliance.subtitle: Regulatory compliance across all jurisdictions and asset classes.
compliance.description: Billion Towers operates under a comprehensive regulatory framework with multiple registrations, audits, and partnerships designed to protect investors and ensure platform integrity.

sections.heading: Regulatory registrations
sections.text: We maintain active registrations with primary financial regulators in all operating jurisdictions.
items: SEC Alternative Trading System (ATS) — Registered as national securities exchange for tokenized securities
FinCEN Money Services Business (MSB) — Licensed for virtual asset transmission and custody
OFAC Sanctions Program — Full compliance screening on all transactions and users
State Money Transmitter Licenses — Registered in all applicable US states and territories
NY BitLicense — Approved for virtual currency activities in New York

sections.heading: AML/KYC compliance
sections.text: Comprehensive anti-money laundering and know-your-customer programs to prevent financial crime.
items: Enhanced customer due diligence (EDD) for all investors above threshold amounts
Continuous transaction monitoring with AI-powered suspicious activity detection
Quarterly CTR (Currency Transaction Report) and SAR (Suspicious Activity Report) filings with FinCEN
Sanctions screening via OFAC SDN list, secondary watchlists, and third-party data providers
Identity verification via government-issued ID, biometric verification, and database matching
PEP (Politically Exposed Person) screening for high-risk users and beneficial owners

sections.heading: Custody and settlement
sections.text: Professional custodians handle all asset settlement and custody with independent auditability.
items: BNY Mellon Pershing — Institutional-grade custody for tokenized securities and cash
Anchorage Digital — Regulated digital asset custody and settlement provider
BitGo — Insurance-backed custody for blockchain-native assets and tokens
Settlement via Ethereum network with redundant blockchain nodes operated independently
Multi-signature custody controls requiring multiple signatures for asset movements

sections.heading: Smart contract audits
sections.text: All platform and asset-related smart contracts undergo rigorous third-party audits.
items: OpenZeppelin — Formal verification and security audit of core platform contracts
CertiK — Continuous security monitoring and blockchain audit of production contracts
Trail of Bits — Advanced adversarial testing and asset-specific contract audits
Public audit reports available for all material contracts via GitHub repositories
Automated monitoring via Forta network for anomalous smart contract behavior

sections.heading: Disclosures and transparency
sections.text: Full transparency into platform operations, financial performance, and risk factors.
items: Form ATS-N filings published quarterly with SEC and market participation details
Annual audited financial statements by Big Four accounting firm (KPMG)
Monthly treasury reports and smart contract audit status published onchain
Risk factor updates whenever material changes affect investor protections
Public disclosure of all partnerships, funding, and material corporate events

sections.heading: Governance and oversight
sections.text: Independent governance structures ensure compliance decisions are protected from commercial pressures.
items: Compliance Committee with external board members and regulatory experts
Annual third-party compliance audit with regulatory scope review
Regular meetings with SEC, FinCEN, and state regulators
Whistleblower program protecting employees reporting compliance concerns
Public incident response policy with mandatory disclosure of security events

how-it-works.title: How Tokenized Real Estate Works
how-it-works.subtitle: Understanding the technology and process behind fractional ownership.
how-it-works.description: Tokenization enables real estate assets to be divided into digital ownership units, traded 24/7 on decentralized networks, with transparent performance tracking and settlement.

sections.heading: Asset tokenization
sections.text: Real estate properties are registered onchain as verified digital representations with embedded economic rights.
items: Property valuation — Independent appraisers assess market value using comparable sales and income approach
Legal documentation — Smart contract encodes ownership rights, dividend streams, and governance rules in immutable code
Blockchain registration — Asset data is registered on Ethereum mainnet with settlement finality
Custody integration — Registered custodian (BNY Mellon, Anchorage) holds asset title and manages distributions
Fractional ownership — Property is divided into millions of individual ownership tokens (e.g., BT-PROP-2026-001 represents one share)

sections.heading: Investor participation
sections.text: Institutional and accredited investors access property opportunities through simple onboarding and transparent pricing.
items: Account setup — KYC verification, accreditation confirmation (SEC Form D), and compliance screening
Wallet connection — Link a custodial wallet (e.g., MetaMask with Institutional custody) or cold storage address
Asset discovery — Browse tokenized properties with full prospectus, financial projections, and risk disclosures
Direct purchase — Buy ownership tokens at transparent NAV prices directly from asset sponsor or secondary market
Instant settlement — Blockchain settlement confirms ownership within minutes; custody is updated immediately

sections.heading: Income distribution
sections.text: Property income (rent, appreciation, dividend, interest) is automatically calculated and distributed to token holders.
items: Cash flow tracking — Property manager submits monthly rental receipts and operating expenses to custodian
Smart contract automation — Dividend contract automatically calculates distributions based on verified cash flows
Stablecoin settlement — Income distributed as USDC (USD Coin), a regulated stablecoin 1:1 backed by USD reserves
Real-time updates — Token holder dashboards update in real-time with accruing income and revised NAV
Tax reporting — Platform generates Form 1099 and K-1 statements for all income distributions

sections.heading: Secondary market trading
sections.text: Token holders can sell holdings on the Billion Towers marketplace or compatible DeFi protocols without asset lock-up periods.
items: Order book trading — Market participants place buy/sell orders; platform matches at fair prices
Automated Market Makers (AMMs) — Liquidity pools enable continuous trading with dynamic pricing
Price discovery — Token prices adjust in real-time based on actual cash flows, market conditions, and demand
Custody preservation — Trading occurs on-custody; custodian updates ownership ledger within hours
Tax efficiency — FIFO and specific lot identification tools help optimize capital gains reporting

sections.heading: Compliance verification
sections.text: Every transaction runs through automated compliance screens to ensure regulatory adherence.
items: Accreditation verification — System confirms investor status (net worth $1M+ or $200K+ income) against federal database
Sanctions screening — OFAC and secondary watchlist checks run on every transaction
Transfer restrictions — Reg D and Rule 144 holdperiods are encoded in smart contracts and enforced automatically
Qualified investor requirements — Certain asset classes restricted to sophisticated investors per offering documents
Geographic compliance — Platform enforces jurisdiction-specific restrictions (OFAC countries, state regulations)

sections.heading: Performance reporting
sections.text: Investors receive monthly performance reports with property updates, financial metrics, and risk assessments.
items: Portfolio dashboard — Real-time view of holdings, NAV, accrued income, and portfolio allocation
Property metrics — Occupancy rates, tenant quality, operating expenses, and maintenance activities
Financial statements — Monthly P&L, quarterly balance sheets, and annual audited financials
Risk metrics — Portfolio concentration, market conditions, regulatory developments, and custodian health
Tax documents — Quarterly estimated tax statements, annual 1099s, and cap gains planning tools
